textContent:
World without Oil
 You know when I was just a little boy; I used to sit down by the fields, not even caring that I will be scorched by the sun as I felt the cool breeze of the countryside that caresses my face. I used to do these kind of things. But due to a turn of events in the world, Nature has been struggling to keep up with what we're doing recently. Additionally, scientists are predicting that the world we live in is going to run out of oil in the next 30 or so years. Now you might think that, "Hey, I'm not a driver. What do I care?" But whether you are or not, oil and gasoline run our daily lives. We wouldn't have fertilizer, machineries need it, and transportation would be like just as we teleported on the 1700s. Again, due to the oil reserves depleting as we speak, we need to make the most out it. Now we are here to optimize gasoline extraction to get more gasoline effectively and to impede the mitigation of Global Warming. These should be used efficiently to prevent things escalating quickly.
	A reason why we will do it this way is that is uses less time, less money and less energy while getting more gasoline than normal extraction methods. Because of this, the customers will bite and stick to our gasoline because of the price. When this dominates the market nationwide, it will increase our sales by fourfold even though it will be a tiny bit cheaper than other types. 
 Another key characteristic that our gasoline features is EcoSave technology. It’s proven by our scientists that it emits less carbon dioxide and it’s suitable for all kinds of cars. Here is a quote from the Paris Agreement“[It] encourages them to focus on reducing emissions at the lowest cost to society; keeping in mind ... improved standards of living worldwide. The best policy options to achieve that goal [is] to promote innovation and technology breakthroughs required to address climate change risks.” Due to this, it fits with the theme of the said agreement. Surely, this feature will totally prevent the worsening of Global Warming.
 Sure, this is good and all but you might think that the cost of production will be expensive. Well yeah, it will be. The equipment and the patent itself will cost a lot of money. But all of the advantages that I said will surely outweigh all the cash we are going to spend. Besides, the capital will be gained back in just a few years. Thus, this investment will be total worth it. 
All in all, we should use our resources better like extracting it effectively and preventing the exacerbation of Global Warming. Don’t you want to feel the breeze that we used to feel when we were kids? Don’t you want to breathe the fresh air not just in the countryside but also in the city? Don’t you want to just lie down in the fields, and not worrying that it’s too hot outside to have a picnic? Don’t you want to do all the things that we used to do but can’t do now? Well, it’s not too late. Join us to make the world a safer place to live for ourselves, your friends, your family, and hopefully the future generation.
title: Persuasive Speech
textContentHtml:
World without Oil

 You know when I was just a little boy; I used to sit down by the fields, not even caring that I will be scorched by the sun as I felt the cool breeze of the countryside that caresses my face. I used to do these kind of things. But due to a turn of events in the world, Nature has been struggling to keep up with what we're doing recently. Additionally, scientists are predicting that the world we live in is going to run out of oil in the next 30 or so years. Now you might think that, "Hey, I'm not a driver. What do I care?" But whether you are or not, oil and gasoline run our daily lives. We wouldn't have fertilizer, machineries need it, and transportation would be like just as we teleported on the 1700s. Again, due to the oil reserves depleting as we speak, we need to make the most out it. Now we are here to optimize gasoline extraction to get more gasoline effectively and to impede the mitigation of Global Warming. These should be used efficiently to prevent things escalating quickly.

A reason why we will do it this way is that is uses less time, less money and less energy while getting more gasoline than normal extraction methods. Because of this, the customers will bite and stick to our gasoline because of the price. When this dominates the market nationwide, it will increase our sales by fourfold even though it will be a tiny bit cheaper than other types. 

 Another key characteristic that our gasoline features is EcoSave technology. It’s proven by our scientists that it emits less carbon dioxide and it’s suitable for all kinds of cars. Here is a quote from the Paris Agreement“[It] encourages them to focus on reducing emissions at the lowest cost to society; keeping in mind ... improved standards of living worldwide. The best policy options to achieve that goal [is] to promote innovation and technology breakthroughs required to address climate change risks.” Due to this, it fits with the theme of the said agreement. Surely, this feature will totally prevent the worsening of Global Warming.

 Sure, this is good and all but you might think that the cost of production will be expensive. Well yeah, it will be. The equipment and the patent itself will cost a lot of money. But all of the advantages that I said will surely outweigh all the cash we are going to spend. Besides, the capital will be gained back in just a few years. Thus, this investment will be total worth it. 

All in all, we should use our resources better like extracting it effectively and preventing the exacerbation of Global Warming. Don’t you want to feel the breeze that we used to feel when we were kids? Don’t you want to breathe the fresh air not just in the countryside but also in the city? Don’t you want to just lie down in the fields, and not worrying that it’s too hot outside to have a picnic? Don’t you want to do all the things that we used to do but can’t do now? Well, it’s not too late. Join us to make the world a safer place to live for ourselves, your friends, your family, and hopefully the future generation.
